task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: Explanation and use of the Printhead Quality check chart

abstract.p: The Printhead Quality check chart (PQCC) prints 2 or 4 prints depending on the configuration.
At the left side a table is positioned with information like:

li.p: Serial numbers of the printheads, installation dates and amount of jetted ink (This is missing in R4.1, known limitation).

abstract.p: In the CMYK configuration, the Printhead Quality Check chart is a four-color gradient print that indicates the condition of the printhead for each color printed.
The gradient is an increasing OD pattern in 8 steps, starting with 10% and ending with a triple wide strip of 100%.
The print is printed in first Gloss High Quality (1 min.) and second Gloss Specialty (2 min.).
In the CMYKW configuration (white ink hardware installed), the Printhead Quality check chart also contains a gradient for the white ink next to the CMYK color.
The third print is printed in White High Quality (4 min.) and second White Specialty (6 min.).

note.p: During printing in the white print mode, only a segment of the printhead (color and white) is used.
So for judging the CMYK printhead quality, use the CMYK chart.

abstract.p: When you print the Printhead Quality check chart you have to select a media.

li.p: The default media settings are used for each of the test prints. They cannot be changed.
The media related settings are applied as defined in the RIP. Wrong settings will lead to PQ artifacts!!

abstract.p: The chart is preceded by 50 cm white space.

li.p: When printed from the leading edge with winding on, additional white space between the 2 blocks is applied.
When printing in 'free fall' mode (winding off), there is no extra white space between the 2 blocks.

section.title: Use of the test print
section.p: The Printhead Quality check chart should be free of visible nozzle failures at normal viewing distances (30 - 50 cm) in specialty print mode.
In exceptional cases, 1 or 2 failing nozzles may be visible in the High Quality Printhead Quality check chart.
This is to be solved by automatic maintenance including nozzle optimization.
In case you suspect a printhead used SDS test 04-6-002 Get nozzle status.
Gloss differences might occur in these prints; gloss banding is not directly related to the separate printheads, but to external aspects like curing and temperature of the print surface.
